meta_description: Instant quotes for chromate conversion coated parts. Ideal for increasing the corrosion resistance of the part while maintaining its conductive properties. RoHS compliant.

sections.heading: Chromate conversion coating services
sections.text: Chromate conversion coating services Chromate conversion coating, more commonly known as chem film or by its brand name Alodine, is a type of conversion coating, used on aluminum as a corrosion inhibitor, as a primer for paint due to increased adherence or to preserve electrical conductivity. Get instant quote

sections.heading: Design considerations for chromate conversion coating:
sections.text: Design considerations for chromate conversion coating: When applying chromate conversion coating, the dimensions of the part can be controlled, enabling it to maintain the requested tolerances.Â Chromate conversion coating can be applied to aluminum alloys, however, steel and iron parts cannot be coated directly.

sections.heading: What is chromate conversion coating?
sections.text: What is chromate conversion coating? Chromate conversion coating is a chemical coating for metal that has many benefits. It protects aluminum from corrosion, serves as a primer for painting and preserves electrical conductivity, making it a useful finish for parts used in the aerospace, automotive and construction industries. At Hubs, we only use chromium-free, RoHS-compliant chromate conversion coating.

sections.heading: How does chromate conversion coating work?
sections.text: How does chromate conversion coating work? Chromate conversion coating involves adding chromic acid to a metal in a chemical process that results in a thin, protective outer layer. This enhances a metalâs durability and makes it more resistant to corrosion. Protolabs Network offers chromate conversion coating for aluminum parts to improve functional properties.
